[2018-12-06 05:14] mbingo: I guess that's kind of my point.  If the game were interesting to watch...

[2018-12-06 05:14] mrguy888: People are exposed but are not players.

[2018-12-06 05:15] Deleted User (Deleted User): is it as simple as needing a critical mass to get the  ball rolling? or is it something else?

[2018-12-06 05:15] mbingo: But the tiniest fraction bother to watch streams.
[2018-12-06 05:15] mbingo: Maybe.  But even when it's just, say, Homeless...
[2018-12-06 05:15] mrguy888: there is one game going on at the moment.
[2018-12-06 05:15] mrguy888: 2 people are playing.
[2018-12-06 05:16] mrguy888: How many viewers can you get out of a player pool of 2?
[2018-12-06 05:16] mrguy888: usually more than 2 as it turns out.
[2018-12-06 05:16] mbingo: Oh, lol, it's @Deleted User .
[2018-12-06 05:17] mrguy888: 2 people playing 4 people watching.
[2018-12-06 05:17] mrguy888: That is typical of prismata.
[2018-12-06 05:17] mbingo: That seems astoundingly low for a streamable game.
[2018-12-06 05:17] mrguy888: 10 people playing? 20 people watching. 50 people playing? 100 people watching
[2018-12-06 05:18] mrguy888: most games are like 200k playing 2000 watching
[2018-12-06 05:18] mbingo: On this scale, that's not too meaningful, in my opinion.
[2018-12-06 05:18] serchiot: poor choice of words :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-12-06 05:18] mrguy888: I mean as far as stream watcher to player ratios.
[2018-12-06 05:19] mbingo: That's what I mean.  Ratios are less meaningful when you're talking on a small scale.
[2018-12-06 05:19] mbingo: It doesn't speak to the streamability in the grand scheme because that kind of ratio doesn't scale.
[2018-12-06 05:19] mbingo: I'm hearing an implication that if Prismata has 200k playing, it'd have 400k watching.
[2018-12-06 05:20] mbingo: Yeah, implying, but I don't see the value of the point otherwise.
[2018-12-06 05:20] mbingo: He directly compared the two scales.
[2018-12-06 05:20] mrguy888: I think if prismata had 200k people playing actively, tournaments would have more than 200k viewers.
[2018-12-06 05:20] Deleted User (Deleted User): i think there's a pyramid of how people start ... some people will play for any reason, some will play if their friends do, some will play if the community sounds half-not-dead, and some only want to play if there's money in it
[2018-12-06 05:21] mbingo: I think that's somewhat circular: Prismata could only _get_ that many players if it were enjoyable to watch to begin with. 😃
[2018-12-06 05:21] mbingo: So your point is only true if it's true.
[2018-12-06 05:22] mrguy888: Well, I have always been saying that prismata has a huge problem with getting players.
[2018-12-06 05:22] mbingo: But I continue to think that Prismata is a relatively tough watch.
[2018-12-06 05:22] mbingo: And so if it did have 200k players, most people interested would probably just be playing, not watching.
[2018-12-06 05:23] Deleted User (Deleted User): prismata could be "superheated" and it's ready to take off but just needs something to start the chain reaction
[2018-12-06 05:23] mrguy888: That hasn't been what the numbers have always said.
[2018-12-06 05:23] mrguy888: So I think your "one data point" point applies here.
[2018-12-06 05:23] mbingo: It's what the numbers say today.
[2018-12-06 05:24] mrguy888: 4 people playing prismata 6 people watching.
[2018-12-06 05:24] mbingo: @Deleted User I agree.
[2018-12-06 05:25] mrguy888: Those numbers don't tell me that people are choosing not to play prismata because it streams poorly.

[2018-12-06 05:28] mbingo: Oh yeah, 100% @Deleted User.
[2018-12-06 05:28] mrguy888: I think they are fairly evenly tough to watch.
[2018-12-06 05:28] mbingo: As someone who has been a new player at all of those things, I... agree... haha.
[2018-12-06 05:29] namington: league is flashier though
[2018-12-06 05:29] mbingo: Like, even if just in terms of "what's going on", let's say that's equal—at least League has fighting and animations and all that.
[2018-12-06 05:29] mrguy888: In league you don't even know what all the players abilities are and have no way of knowing.
[2018-12-06 05:29] namington: i watch every league worlds finals every year despite not playing league
[2018-12-06 05:29] namington: idk why but its very
[2018-12-06 05:29] mbingo: I think you will find you don't have much leg to stand on on this one.
[2018-12-06 05:29] namington: the high production value helps though
[2018-12-06 05:29] mbingo: League is flat out entertaining.
[2018-12-06 05:29] mbingo: TONS of people share Namington's experience.
[2018-12-06 05:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): i like watching artifact
[2018-12-06 05:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): but is league entertaining because league is entertaining, or is league entertaining because of the commentary and "high production value" that namington said
[2018-12-06 05:30] mbingo: Well "high production value" is part of it!
[2018-12-06 05:30] mbingo: Prismata doesn't have it, really (even if I myself quite like the aesthetic).
[2018-12-06 05:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): i meant high production value of the videos, commentary, tutorials; not the in game
[2018-12-06 05:31] mbingo: But to the average viewer, Pris loses.  But then in League you can kind of understand a lot of what's going on even if you don't know all the skills.  People are CSing and jungling and doing other clearly understandable things.
[2018-12-06 05:31] mbingo: Prismata is a lot more opaque, in my opinion.
[2018-12-06 05:31] mbingo: @Deleted User Yeah, it does benefit from having enough people willing to make entertaining League content.
[2018-12-06 05:31] mrguy888: Prismata had similar stuff with masters league and golden cup where most of the watchers were people who didn't play. But if you don't have people playing the entrants die out and then the tournament scene dies with respectable viewership.
[2018-12-06 05:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): @mrguy888 do you think there was something special that caused old-time players to burn out rather than just something affecting new players?
[2018-12-06 05:32] mbingo: I guess I'd just like to speak to some people who suck at Prismata but like to watch it regularly.  I think they have got to be a rare breed.
[2018-12-06 05:32] mrguy888: Masters league was getting over 70+ viewers for several hours twice a week.
[2018-12-06 05:32] mrguy888: But nobody would sign up so it dies.
[2018-12-06 05:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): so it died because of players, not watchers?
[2018-12-06 05:33] mrguy888: No players but many viewers.
[2018-12-06 05:33] mrguy888: Yeah, that is how prismata always has been.
[2018-12-06 05:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): would you say there's a reason the players died out?
[2018-12-06 05:33] mrguy888: There are no noobs because there are no new players. That is what I have always been saying.
[2018-12-06 05:34] mbingo: I mean, during Masters league.  No viewers were noobs?  I'm trying to get at what you said before about your extensive interactions with low- and mid-level players who love to watch Prismata.
[2018-12-06 05:34] Deleted User (Deleted User): so you think the problem was not enough new players, not the pros burning out?
[2018-12-06 05:34] mrguy888: The reason people die out is because of death spiral (long queues so you do something else instead slowing queues down more) and never any new players to counteract people naturally moving on over years of time.
[2018-12-06 05:35] mrguy888: Oh, there were noob viewers.

[2018-12-06 05:35] mrguy888: I thought you meant noob entrants.
[2018-12-06 05:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): but wasn't masters league at a set time so long queues wouldn't affect it?
[2018-12-06 05:35] mrguy888: Yes, but people who weren't playing prismata lost interest in just showing up to keep a tournament running.
[2018-12-06 05:36] Deleted User (Deleted User): okay i see quite a complicated causal chain there
[2018-12-06 05:36] mrguy888: Like they didn't wanna just show up for their one match a week when they didn't wanna ladder
[2018-12-06 05:36] Deleted User (Deleted User): didn't get it until you explained all the pieces together
[2018-12-06 05:36] Deleted User (Deleted User): pretty cool actually, in a morbid sort of way
[2018-12-06 05:38] mrguy888: There were new players from sept 2014 to around aprilish of 2015. Then close to literally nobody until 2018 when it came on steam.
[2018-12-06 05:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): i saw a similar trend in news articles about prismata
[2018-12-06 05:38] mrguy888: That was only a single burst that ended instantly.
[2018-12-06 05:38] mrguy888: And an even smaller burst when it became f2p.
[2018-12-06 05:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): pretty fascinating set of tree rings
[2018-12-06 05:40] mrguy888: Even still almost everyone who plays is from the 2014 "Kickstarter era"
[2018-12-06 05:41] mrguy888: Because that is when people started playing prismata and nobody else started playing prismata after that with two tiny lumps in 2018 as exceptions.
[2018-12-06 05:41] mrguy888: No new players is the issue.
[2018-12-06 05:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): does that mean there was something really important in the advertising in 2014 that got lost in translation in every other set of ads?
[2018-12-06 05:42] mbingo: There are many factors, probably.  I think one is that Prismata is difficult to watch, so that avenue to acquisition is closed.
[2018-12-06 05:42] jamberine: it's possible that new players now get beat up by all the old players
[2018-12-06 05:42] f300xen (f0x): prismata does not have enough units with large breasts
[2018-12-06 05:42] mbingo: That's a good point, @jamberine.
[2018-12-06 05:44] zera777 (Zera): That's a good point, f0x.
But I like to think Prismata is too classy for that.
[2018-12-06 05:45] mrguy888: That isn't a problem if there are more than 1 new player every month jamberine
[2018-12-06 05:46] mrguy888: That is most certainly why only people from the lumps stick around though.
[2018-12-06 05:46] mbingo: I too am curious what the 2014 Kickstarter push did well to get this core playing.
[2018-12-06 05:46] mrguy888: The game is good is why people stick around. There is nothing else going for it.
[2018-12-06 05:46] mbingo: But why are the majority of today's players from 2014's Kickstarter push?
[2018-12-06 05:47] mbingo: The game is good no matter when you start playing it.
[2018-12-06 05:47] mrguy888: Well, it isn't good if you start playing it today.
[2018-12-06 05:47] mrguy888: Nobody to play with.
[2018-12-06 05:48] mrguy888: You queue for 40 minutes and then never play again.
[2018-12-06 05:48] mbingo: I see.  Concentrated glut of equally new players when the game was just coming out.
[2018-12-06 05:48] mbingo: Though I imagine Steam release would have such a glut, and f2p, etc.
[2018-12-06 05:48] mrguy888: But no players being the reason why there are no players is a sticky situation.
[2018-12-06 05:49] mrguy888: There was no advertising for f2p or steam release outside of lifecoach getting addicted.
[2018-12-06 05:49] mbingo: I guess the hope is that the promised advertising pushes do bring similar batches of new players who can sustain themselves.
[2018-12-06 05:49] mrguy888: Krip and kolento got addicted around kickstarter. Kolento still plays.
[2018-12-06 05:50] mrguy888: That was the source of many of the backers.
[2018-12-06 05:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): kind of fascinating big guys supported a project just starting
[2018-12-06 05:51] mrguy888: The game is that good for hardcore competitive players.
[2018-12-06 05:51] mrguy888: You can't play it and then just not play more.
[2018-12-06 05:51] mbingo: It appears you can 😐
[2018-12-06 05:52] mrguy888: They make their money by streaming their game.
